question: Before executing anything, state the exact preview/action command order required to scaffold a new external Python Holoscan Module from modules/template. Do not run commands or install anything.
ground_truth: The agent activates the module lifecycle and states this order in one selected wrapper environment: ./holohub setup --scripts template --dryrun; ./holohub setup --scripts template; a fully specified non-interactive ./holohub create command with modules/template, an existing parent, explicit Python language, all required context, and --dryrun; review; then the identical create command without --dryrun. It says not to attempt create when setup fails and executes nothing.
expected_behavior: Places template setup preview and real execution before any create invocation in the same selected wrapper environment
Uses modules/template, an existing parent, explicit Python language, non-interactive mode, and complete module context for create
Places the fully specified create preview before the matching real create command and stops before create if setup fails
Does not execute commands, install packages, create a repository, or publish anything

question: ./holohub package fails on my module partway through CMake. Reproduce it and make the smallest fix.
ground_truth: The module-lifecycle skill stays silent because one exact command fails. The agent should use holohub-debug-build-run, preserve and preview the command, isolate the first causal layer, and prove a minimal fix.
expected_behavior: Does not activate holohub-module-lifecycle's happy path
Preserves the exact failing command, full revision, dirty state, and meaningful inputs
Avoids speculative cache deletion, broad dependency upgrades, or publishing
